nl.statement: Deterministic triangular-array collapsed cohort-by-time Poisson-projection world: cohorts g in C encoded as WithTop (Fin T) (never-treated = top), calendar periods t in Fin T, cohort-time cells carry positive masses q_gt / q_gtN, untreated cohort means B_gt = bar_b_g*exp(gamma_t0), observed cohort means m_gt(delta) = B_gt*exp(D_gt*delta_gt), collapsed regressor r_gt in R^(|C|+T), and the Poisson pseudo-criteria L_Nc / L. This is the ambient world of every headline statement (all six operate on the limiting/finite-array collapsed quantities). — bypass-justified: Causalean has NO Poisson/PPML world (grep 'Poisson|PPML|expMean|multiplicative' over Causalean/ returns only unrelated concentration/minimax/ML modules; grep over Causalean/Panel/ returns 0). The nonlinear pseudo-likelihood criteria L_Nc/L, their unique maximizers, strict concavity/coercivity, and the fitted means mu_star are all bespoke finite-dimensional convex analysis built over Mathlib (Fin, WithTop, Real.exp, StrictConcaveOn, IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn). REUSED building blocks: the cohort/never-treated encoding is Causalean.Panel.AdoptionPath (WithTop (Fin T), le/lt/isNeverTreated, verified present); the mean-weighted-FWL residual layer (see def:weighted-fwl-residual) reuses Causalean.Panel.Weighted.WeightedSupport.{tildeX,Q_XX,thetaHat,RankCondition} (Subspace/FWL), and thm:sharp additionally reuses WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs (ScalarFWL.lean:74) for the normal-equation FWL coefficient ratio in the IFT derivative step. No single Causalean structure realizes this world, so the world skeleton itself is define-local with those helpers imported.

Finite-N unit-and-time fixed-effect Poisson panel with potential outcomes: units i in Fin N with deterministic cohort labels G_i in C (n_gN units per cohort), unit baselines b_iN>0, unit-time regressor v_itN in R^(N+T) (intercept, unit-FE, time-FE, treatment D_it), unit-time observed mean m_itN(delta)=b_iN*exp(gamma_t0+D_it*delta_{G_i,t}), the unit-FE Poisson criterion L_N and its beta coordinate beta_Nstar, and the extended-MLE sample coefficient hat_beta_N. Potential outcomes Y_it(d) and the array sampling law Pcal_N with expectation E_Pcal_N enter ONLY through the two mean-defining assumptions. — bypass-justified: honest_scope disclaims all sampling/inference content (no fixed-T consistency, influence function, variance, or coverage claim). The probability layer is therefore not needed at theorem level: the two PO assumptions (ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean, ass:proportional-effects) pin E_Pcal_N[Y_it(d)] to the deterministic mean m_itN(delta), and every downstream statement consumes m_itN, not the law. E_Pcal_N is treated as an abstract linear expectation functional / the means as primitives over Mathlib (no MeasureTheory import required). REUSED: unit-time assignment/history from Causalean.Panel.PO.TreatmentPath and cohort encoding from Causalean.Panel.AdoptionPath. Causalean's PO probability spaces are at a heavier (measure-theoretic) abstraction than this purely-algebraic mean-level statement, so binding is define-local.
For every N>=|C| and every g in C, n_gN is positive and n_gN/N converges to pi_g in (0,1).

review.note: After unfolding cohortCount and cohortShare, this requires positive supported cohort counts for every N≥|C| and convergence n_gN/N→pi_g for every g∈C, with pi_g intrinsically in (0,1); 0<N is redundant under the standing nonempty supported-cohort setup.

standard.name: positive limiting cohort shares
standard.citation: Clement de Chaisemartin and Xavier D'Haultfoeuille (2020), Two-Way Fixed Effects Estimators with Heterogeneous Treatment Effects, American Economic Review 110(9), 2964-2996, https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.20181169.

nl.statement: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)]=b_iN exp(gamma_t0) for every i and t, with gamma_10=0.

review.note: After unfolding expectationUnder, the equality is imposed for every N,i,t, and the Fin-index condition t.val=0 exactly expresses the paper normalization gamma_10=0 under zero-indexed Lean periods.

standard.name: unit-fixed-effect multiplicative parallel trends in an exponential mean
standard.citation: Jeffrey M. Wooldridge (2023), Simple Approaches to Nonlinear Difference-in-Differences with Panel Data, The Econometrics Journal 26(3), C31-C66, https://doi.org/10.1093/ectj/utad016.

nl.statement: For every g in C, the deterministic triangular-array average bar_b_gN=n_gN^(-1)sum_{i in I_gN}b_iN converges to bar_b_g in (0,infinity).

review.note: Unfolding withinCohortBaseline gives n_gN⁻¹ times the sum of b_iN over I_gN, and the predicate requires its convergence for every g∈C to barB g, whose PosReal type exactly enforces a limit in (0,∞).

nl.statement: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(1)]=E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)] exp(delta_{G_i,t}) for every i and t with D_it=1.

review.note: Unfolding unitTreatment and expectationUnder yields exactly the treated-cell implication D_it=1 ⇒ E[Y_it(1)]=E[Y_it(0)]exp(delta_{G_i,t}) for every N,i,t.

standard.name: cohort-time proportional treatment effects
standard.citation: Ninon Moreau-Kastler (2025), Proportional Treatment Effects in Staggered Settings: An Approach for Poisson Pseudo-Maximum Likelihood, EU Tax Observatory Working Paper No. 31, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.5247936.

nl.statement: sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite.

review.note: Unfolding limitingCellMass, collapsedRegressor, and collapsedIndex shows the predicate is exactly strict positivity of a'(∑_{g,t}q_gt r_gt r_gt')a for every nonzero collapsed parameter a, equivalent to positive definiteness.

standard.name: full-rank Poisson fixed-effect design
standard.citation: Halbert White (1982), Maximum Likelihood Estimation of Misspecified Models, Econometrica 50(1), 1-25, https://doi.org/10.2307/1912526.

review.note: Lean calendar indices are zero-based, so witnesses with values 1, 2, and 3 are precisely paper cohorts 2, 3, and 4, while ⊤ is the never-treated infinity cohort.
Unfolding treatmentIndicator gives D_gt, and the definition requires delta_gt>0 exactly for every supported cohort-time cell with D_gt=1.

nl.statement: beta_Nstar(delta) is the last coordinate of argmax_{theta_N in R^(N+T)} L_N(theta_N;delta), where L_N(theta_N;delta)=(NT)^(-1)sum_{i=1}^Nsum_{t=1}^T[m_itN(delta)v_itN'theta_N-exp(v_itN'theta_N)].

review.note: After unfolding unitCriterion, UnitParameter, unitRegressor, unitObservedMean, and maximizerOrZero, betaNStar is the final coordinate of a selected global maximizer of exactly the normalized unit-and-time-FE population Poisson criterion.

nl.statement: theta_star(delta)=argmax_{theta in R^(|C|+T)}L(theta;delta), L(theta;delta)=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt[m_gt(delta)r_gt'theta-exp(r_gt'theta)], beta_star(delta)=the last coordinate of theta_star(delta), and mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta)).

review.note: After unfolding limitingCriterion, CollapsedParameter, collapsedRegressor, and maximizerOrZero, this selects theta_star from the stated collapsed argmax; companion definitions betaStar and fittedMean give its last coordinate and exp(r_gt' theta_star) exactly.

nl.statement: Let A_N be the argmax of sum_{i=1}^Nsum_{t=1}^T[Y_it v_itN'theta_N-exp(v_itN'theta_N)] over unit-effect coordinates in R union {-infinity}, time-effect coordinates in R with the period-1 normalization, and beta in R, using exp(-infinity)=0. If A_N contains a point with finite beta, hat_beta_N is its beta coordinate after choosing the finite (time-effect,beta) coordinates of smallest Euclidean norm; otherwise hat_beta_N=0.
nl.frozen_body:
Let \(A_N\) be the set of admissible extended maximizers of
\[
\sum_{i=1}^N\sum_{t=1}^T
\left\{
Y_{it}\,v_{itN}'\theta_N-\exp(v_{itN}'\theta_N)
\right\}
\]
over unit-effect coordinates in \(\mathbb R\cup\{-\infty\}\), time-effect coordinates in \(\mathbb R\) satisfying the period-1 normalization, and \(\beta\in\mathbb R\), with the convention \(\exp(-\infty)=0\). Admissibility means that the period-1 time effect is normalized to zero and that a unit-effect coordinate may equal \(-\infty\) only for a unit whose whole observed outcome path satisfies \(Y_{it}=0\) for every \(t\); for such a unit the extended objective contribution is defined to be zero. If \(A_N\) contains an element whose squared Euclidean norm over the finite time-effect and \(\beta\) coordinates is no larger than that of every element of \(A_N\), then \(\hat\beta_N\) is the \(\beta\)-coordinate of such a selected element. If no such minimum-finite-coordinate-norm admissible extended maximizer exists, set \(\hat\beta_N=0\).

review.note: Option ℝ realizes finite unit effects plus minus infinity, admissibility implements the normalized extended objective, and hatBetaN selects a global maximizer minimizing the squared Euclidean norm of the finite time-and-beta coordinates with the stated zero fallback.

nl.statement: rho_star(delta)=argmin_{rho in R^(|C|+T-1)}sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt mu_star_gt(delta)[D_gt-X_gt'rho]^2 and Wtilde_gt(delta)=D_gt-X_gt'rho_star(delta).

review.note: weightedFWLResidual = c.tildeX (X_gt span) D over meanWeightedSupport whose weights meanFWLWeight = limitingCellMass·fittedMean = q_gt·μ*_gt(δ); this is the weighted FWL residual D_gt − X_gt'ρ*, with weightedFWLResidual_eq_rhoStar tying it to the argmin ρ*. Both directions hold.

nl.statement: R_T={((pi_g)_{g in C},(B_gt)_{g in C,1<=t<=T},(delta_gt)_{(g,t):D_gt=1}): C contains {2,3,4,infinity}; B_gt=bar_b_g exp(gamma_t0); delta_gt>0 whenever D_gt=1; sum_{g,t}q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite; and beta_star(delta)<0}.
nl.frozen_body:
Define \(\mathcal R_T\) as the set of all triples
\[
\left((\pi_g)_{g\in\mathcal C},(B_{gt})_{g\in\mathcal C,\ 1\le t\le T},(\delta_{gt})_{(g,t):D_{gt}=1}\right)
\]
with \(T\ge 4\), indexed by a cohort support \(\mathcal C\) that contains \(\{2,3,4,\infty\}\) and contains no first-period finite cohort, such that
\[
0<\pi_g<1\quad(g\in\mathcal C),\qquad \sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\pi_g=1,
\]
there exist \(\bar b_g>0\) for \(g\in\mathcal C\) with \(B_{gt}=\bar b_g\exp(\gamma_{t0})\) for every supported cell \((g,t)\), \(\delta_{gt}>0\) for every treated supported cell \((g,t)\) with \(D_{gt}=1\), the collapsed design has full weighted rank in the sense that
\[
\forall a\ne 0,\qquad
0<\sum_{g\in\mathcal C}\sum_{t=1}^T \frac{\pi_g}{T}\,(r_{gt}'a)^2,
\]
and
\[
\beta^\star(\delta)<0.
\]

review.note: All note conjuncts encoded: MulticohortFrontierScope gives C⊇{2,3,4,∞}, ∃barB with B_gt=barB·exp(γ_t), δ>0 on TreatedSupportedCell, ∀a≠0 Σ(π_g/T)(r_gt'a)²>0 (= Σq_gt r r' pos-def), primitiveBetaStar<0 (=β* since primitiveLimitingCriterion=limitingCriterion). Σπ_g=1 is the intrinsic limiting-share normalization; ValidPanelHorizon/ValidCohortSupport are standing setup — regularity-bookkeeping, not a strengthening.

nl.statement: W4 is the cofinal triangular subsequence indexed by N=4m, m=1,2,..., with T=4, C={2,3,4,infinity}, n_2N=n_3N=n_4N=n_infinityN=m, b_iN=1 for every i, gamma_t0=0 for every t, delta_gt=log(101/100) for every treated cell except delta_2,4=log(4).

review.note: fourCohortSupport={1,2,3,⊤}=paper {2,3,4,∞}; counts m+1 with N=4(m+1) is a cofinal reindex of paper N=4m/count m (equal shares 1/4); baselines 1, γ=0, δ=log 4 at Lean cell (1,3)=paper (2,4) and log(101/100) at other treated cells. T=4 baked into Cohort 4. Equivalent.

nl.statement: Define the nuisance-free frontier-elimination polynomial Phi(delta) = M A - sum_{g,t} D_gt R_g C_t, where h_gt = pi_g B_gt exp(D_gt delta_gt), R_g = sum_t h_gt, C_t = sum_g h_gt, M = sum_g R_g, and A = sum_{g,t} D_gt h_gt. The relation between Phi and beta_star(delta) is stated separately by the primitive global frontier theorem.

review.note: Phi = primitiveTotal·primitiveTreatedTotal − Σ D_gt·primitiveRow_g·primitiveColumn_t = M·A − Σ D_gt R_g C_t, the row/column-margin elimination polynomial in primitiveH=π_g·m_gt only (mu_star nuisance eliminated); faithfully names the nuisance-free frontier handle the note's procedure produces (the β*=0 characterization is a separate theorem). Common positive T-scaling does not change the zero-set.

nl.statement: For every N, the unit-and-time-FE population projection in def:unit-fe-population-projection has a unique beta_Nstar(delta). Its beta_Nstar(delta) equals the beta coordinate of the unique maximizer of L_Nc(theta;delta). Consequently, beta_Nstar(delta) converges to beta_star(delta). Thus within-cohort baseline heterogeneity (b_iN) enters the limiting unit-FE coefficient only through B_gtN=n_gN^(-1)sum_{i in I_gN}b_iN exp(gamma_t0).

review.note: Lean conjunction faithfully encodes the note: unique unit-FE (L_N) maximizer and unique collapsed (L_Nc) maximizer with betaNStar = its beta coord (clause A), betaNStar → betaStar (clause B), and 'b_iN enters only through B_gtN' as the invariance clause C (equal withinCohortBaseline on C ⇒ equal betaNStar, since B_gtN = bar_b_gN·exp(gamma_t0) with gamma fixed). Hypotheses = ass:cohort-share-limit/unit-untreated-exponential-mean/within-cohort-baseline-limit/proportional-effects/collapsed-design-rank plus standing horizon/support setup; equivalent both ways.

nl.statement: The limiting projection theta_star(delta) is unique and satisfies sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt r_gt[m_gt(delta)-mu_star_gt(delta)]=0.

review.note: IsUniqueGlobalMax encodes 'theta_star unique'; the vector score ∑ q_gt r_gt[m_gt−mu*_gt]=0 is faithfully decomposed coordinate-wise into the nuisance-index conjunct and the treatment-coordinate conjunct (r_gt = (collapsedNuisanceRegressor, treatmentIndicator)), with limitingCellMass=q_gt, observedCohortMean=m_gt, fittedMean=exp(r_gt'theta*)=mu*_gt. Equivalent both ways; hRank = ass:collapsed-design-rank supplies uniqueness.

nl.statement: For the limiting coefficient beta_star(delta) of the unit-and-time-FE Poisson projection, at every delta satisfying the assumptions and for every treated cell (k,s), beta_star(delta) is differentiable in delta_ks and d beta_star(delta)/d delta_ks=q_ks B_ks exp(delta_ks) Wtilde_ks(delta)/[sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt mu_star_gt(delta)Wtilde_gt(delta)^2]. The denominator is strictly positive. Hence sign{d beta_star(delta)/d delta_ks}=sign{Wtilde_ks(delta)}, and a treated cell is a forbidden comparison cell if and only if Wtilde_ks(delta)<0. Both Wtilde and its sign depend on delta through mu_star.

review.note: Lean ⟺ note both ways. HasDerivAt of (fun x => betaStar … (update delta (k,s) x)) at delta(k,s) with derivative = limitingCellMass k (=q_ks=pi_k/T) * untreatedMean k s (=B_ks=barB_k·exp gamma_s) * exp(delta(k,s)) * W / fwlEnergy encodes exactly d beta_star/d delta_ks = q_ks B_ks exp(delta_ks) Wtilde_ks / [Σ q_gt mu*_gt Wtilde_gt²]; W=weightedFWLResidual=Wtilde and denominator fwlEnergy=Σ limitingCellMass·fittedMean(=mu*)·Wtilde² match def:weighted-fwl-residual and def:collapsed-population-projection. 0<denominator gives strict positivity; the three iffs (derivative<0↔W<0, =0↔=0, >0↔>0) are exactly sign{d beta}=sign{Wtilde} and forbidden ⟺ Wtilde<0. hks:treatmentIndicator=1 encodes 'treated cell', hRank:CollapsedDesignRank is the pos-def collapsed design (ass:collapsed-design-rank); remaining paper assumptions are baked into the constructive limiting objects. No weakening in either direction.

nl.statement: The explicitly constructed W4 belongs to R_4. In particular, every treated cell has a strictly positive proportional effect, the exceptional cell (2,4) has the largest effect, and beta_star(delta)<0. At b_iN=1 and delta_gt=0 for all treated cells, Wtilde_gt equals the matrix with rows g=2,3,4,infinity and columns t=1,2,3,4: [(-3,3,1,-1),(-1,-3,3,1),(1,-1,-3,3),(3,1,-1,-3)]/8. Thus Wtilde_2,4=-1/8 and d beta_star(delta)/d delta_2,4<0 at the homogeneous no-effect point; continuity gives an open positive-effect neighborhood in which increasing the late effect of the earliest cohort strictly lowers beta_star.

review.note: Unfolding the region, witness, residual, and positivity definitions gives the same W4 membership, unique largest positive (2,4) effect, full residual matrix, exact negative derivative (-1/10), and an open all-positive neighborhood with negative late-cell derivative.

nl.statement: If delta_gt=delta_0 for every treated cell, then beta_star(delta)=delta_0 and mu_star_gt(delta)=B_gt exp(D_gt delta_0).

review.note: Unfolding betaStar, fittedMean, untreatedMean, and treatmentIndicator, the hypothesis is exactly delta_gt=delta_0 on every treated supported cell and the conclusion is exactly beta_star(delta)=delta_0 and mu_star_gt(delta)=B_gt exp(D_gt delta_0); hMean supplies the standing time-effect normalization and hRank is the paper's stated collapsed-design-rank assumption.

nl.statement: For h_gt=pi_g B_gt exp(D_gt delta_gt), define R_g=sum_{t=1}^T h_gt, C_t=sum_{g in C}h_gt, M=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T h_gt, A=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T D_gt h_gt, and Phi=M A-sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T D_gt R_g C_t. Then sign{beta_star(delta)}=sign(Phi). In particular beta_star(delta)=0 if and only if Phi=0, so the requested zero-level set is the finite primitive system Phi>=0 and -Phi>=0, with no pseudo-true intercept, cohort, or time multiplier retained; subject to the defining positive-effect, baseline, share, and rank restrictions, R_T is exactly Phi<0. In the T=4, C={2,3,4,infinity}, equal-share, unit-baseline subfamily having treated multiplier x>1 outside (2,4) and multiplier y>1 at (2,4), Phi=(5x^2+12x-2y-15)/16, so beta_star<0 if and only if y>(5x^2+12x-15)/2; at x=101/100 the sharp cutoff is 4441/4000, and W4 has y=4. Operationally, let H={(g,t):D_gt=1}, Bobs_gt=m_g1 m_infinity,t/m_infinity,1, Z=sum_H q_gt Bobs_gt, omega_gt=q_gt Bobs_gt/Z, tau_gt=m_gt/Bobs_gt-1, and PTT=sum_H omega_gt tau_gt. Under the multiplicative-parallel-trends and proportional-effect assumptions, Bobs_gt=B_gt, tau_gt=exp(delta_gt)-1, every omega_gt is positive and the weights sum to one, and PTT=(sum_H q_gt m_gt)/(sum_H q_gt Bobs_gt)-1. Thus with positive effects, Phi<0 implies beta_star<0<PTT: Phi diagnoses the pooled PPML coefficient as an unsafe causal summary, while granular tau_gt and their counterfactual-share PTT form a distinct identified causal target, not a correction or reinterpretation of beta_star. This population result makes no fixed-T sampling or inference claim for hat_beta_N.

review.note: After unfolding Phi, betaStar, R_T, the four-cohort fixture, Bobs, omega, tau, and PTT, the Lean conjunction is mathematically equivalent to the paper: it gives all three sign cases, the exact Phi<0 region, the T=4 polynomial/cutoff and W4 value, and the counterfactual-share identities and positivity diagnosis under the stated standing restrictions.
